title: Lay out vertex and buffer data for the GPU
description: Pack attributes and uniforms to honor alignment rules and cache locality so the GPU fetches data efficiently and correctly.
body: Use when defining vertex attributes, uniform/storage buffers, or push constants. Do not ignore std140/std430 alignment (vec3 padded to 16 bytes), and avoid scattered per-attribute buffers when interleaving improves locality. Actions: match the CPU struct layout to the shader's expected alignment, interleave hot attributes, and validate offsets with the reflection/validation layer. Validation signals include matching CPU/GPU struct sizes and correct values read in the shader. Risk boundary: a silent alignment mismatch reads garbage uniforms and renders subtly wrong output; verify buffer offsets via reflection rather than assuming the compiler agreed.
